steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The noun phrase “an ecological [blank]” is followed by a verb phrase “compared the lengths of eggs...” that modifies or describes the study. To properly connect this verb phrase to the noun, a relative pronoun is needed.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive relative clause rule
steps.content: Relative clauses that identify or specify a noun are restrictive and use “that” without a comma. Option C (“study that”) correctly forms a restrictive relative clause. The other three either add a non-standard comma before “that” (A), omit the relative pronoun entirely (B), or use “which” with a comma (D), which is for non-restrictive clauses. That matches choice C — C is correct.
